creator: bsdcode@disroot.org
text:
Created attachment 248584
patch

Upstream's release cycle can be long, one to two years. For some users it might be beneficial to have a port which tracks the development version, e.g. if the OpenAL backend in the current release version is bugged, the SDL2_mixer backend is not quite a good replacement and it is unknown when the next release which fixes the problems with OpenAL is coming. Therefore I am preparing a new port for the development version.

Of course the new devel port and this port should be in sync. Because we need to get the development checkout from GitHub I switch the mastersite for this port from SourceForge to GitHub. In my opinion it also makes the port a little bit cleaner, because we can ditch the MASTER_SITES, DISTNAME and WRKSRC setting in the Makefile.

When I changed to USE_GITHUB I got a mismatch in the pkg-plist. It turns out, the source tarball from SourceForge bundles two audio assets for the demo which are not included in the GitHub repository. Upstream has outsourced them into a separate repository and, obviously, includes them in the SourceForge tarball. So I added the asset's distfile to the port, introduced a option to disable the inclusion of the two audio files and added a post-extract target which puts the files into place.

Updated patch notes:

- use GCC (https://github.com/gemrb/gemrb/issues/1786)
- add optional OpenGL and SDL2_mixer support
- add disabled pixelscaling to screensize option
- change GemRB.cfg into a proper conffile
  (remove from pkg-plist and annotate GemRB.cfg.sample with @sample)
- fix and modernize pkg-message
- use distfiles form GitHub instead of SourceForge
- make PNG, OGG, TTF and OpenAL support optional
- make optional demo assets also optional in the port
- use compiler:c++11-lang
- remove gnome and localbase uses
- remove DOC_DIR and MAN_DIR from CMAKE_ARGS
- set CMake options explicitly
- use CMake options helpers for boolean options
- organize options into AUDIO, RESOURCE and VIDEO groups
